title: MP School Summer Vacation 2026 Extended: Teachers to Rejoin on June 7, Students to Return from June 16; Check Government, Private, Army, Air Force & Central Schools Holiday Updates Amid Heatwave - The Sunday Guardian
full_text:
Government schools are expected to follow the revised schedule, while private, Army, Air Force, CBSE, ICSE, and central schools may issue institution-specific updates.
The Madhya Pradesh government has extended the summer vacation for teachers by one week due to the severe heatwave conditions being reported across several districts of the state. Teachers will now return to schools on June 7, 2026, while students will continue to resume classes on June 16, 2026, as per the revised schedule.
The decision was taken after temperatures crossed 45°C in multiple regions, raising concerns over health and safety amid the ongoing extreme weather conditions.
According to the School Education Department, many districts in Madhya Pradesh are experiencing intense heatwave conditions with temperatures exceeding 45°C. Authorities stated that teachers are also engaged in census-related work and board examination duties, which were considered while extending the vacation period.
The revised vacation schedule aims to protect teaching staff from extreme heat exposure while ensuring schools are prepared before the new academic session begins.
The state government has confirmed that Dussehra, Diwali, and winter vacation schedules will remain unchanged.
The revised schedule is primarily applicable to government schools under the Madhya Pradesh School Education Department. Reports indicate that recognised educational institutions across the state are expected to follow the updated academic calendar.
Government schools across Madhya Pradesh will reopen for students from June 16, while teachers will report from June 7.
Many private schools generally align their vacation schedules with state government notifications. However, students and parents should verify reopening dates directly with their respective schools, as individual institutions may issue separate circulars.
No separate nationwide notification has been issued regarding vacation extension for CBSE-affiliated, ICSE, or central schools. Schools operating under their own management may follow institution-specific calendars unless local authorities announce additional measures.
As of now, there is no official announcement regarding a separate extension of summer vacations for Army Public Schools or Air Force Schools in Madhya Pradesh. Parents are advised to check directly with their respective institutions for updated reopening schedules. Army schools' vacation will begin on 1 June as per the army school calendar till 30 June. Airforce school summer break was from 29 May to 30 June for the senior wing & junior wing.
The state remains under intense heat stress, with several districts recording temperatures above 45°C. Earlier, some districts had already revised school timings to reduce exposure during peak afternoon hours. Authorities continue to monitor weather conditions and may issue further advisories if required.
Along with the reopening of schools, the second phase of the School Chalen Hum campaign will start from June 16 and continue till June 30. The initiative aims to improve student enrolment, bring back dropouts, and ensure the smooth commencement of the academic session.
Hence, the decision was taken after temperatures crossed 45°C in several districts, prompting authorities to prioritise health and safety.
